interactions.summary: Jiang reads Bernard's prayer as a radical elevation of Dante: a mortal must imagine God and preserve that vision as memory, while even angels burn to know what Dante can know.
He explains Dante's vision of God as overwhelming, pre-Big-Bang interconnected substance, perfect goodness, and a memory reconstructed years later through imagination.
Jiang follows Dante's jigsaw assembly into the Holy Trinity, then identifies the shock: within the Trinity Dante sees the human effigy, meaning humanity is inside God.

speaker_notes.note: This packet repeatedly alternates between quoted Dante lines and Jiang's interpretation.
speaker_notes.suggested_speaker: Jiang reading and interpreting quoted material

speaker_notes.note: Jiang asks 'Any questions so far?' and 'Are we clear?' but no student answer is captured.

claims.claim: Jiang says Dante's higher vision means God must be imagined through will, not seen directly.
He reads Bernard's desire for Dante's vision as revolutionary because angels lack access to a truth available to the mortal poet.
Jiang links Dante's imagination-to-memory sequence to modern neuroscience: humans imagine the world, turn it into story, and that story becomes memory.
He interprets Dante's vision of God as the universe before the Big Bang, a chaotic pool of substances and relations before creation disperses them into our universe.
Jiang says Dante slowly assembles the vision into the Holy Trinity: three circles that are separate but the same.
The deepest secret Jiang identifies is that Dante sees a human effigy inside God, meaning humanity is within the essence of God despite Augustine's separation of sinful humans from divine goodness.
claims.temporal_scope: Central interpretive claim in this lecture.

signature_moments.moment: Only Dante, a mortal man, can give angels access to truth.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Only Dante, a mortal man, has access to truth
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It reverses the hierarchy between heaven and human imagination.

signature_moments.moment: Memory is imagination hardened into story.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you turn the world into a story. And the story becomes your memory
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It connects Dante's poetic theology to Jiang's model of cognition.

signature_moments.moment: God is imagined as the universe before the universe, a pre-Big-Bang unity.
signature_moments.source_phrase: God as the universe before the universe
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives Dante's mystical vision a cosmological image.

signature_moments.moment: Inside the Holy Trinity is a mirror image of us.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Within the Holy Trinity there is an image of us
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the puzzle's central reversal: humans are not outside God.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's description of the sequence by which a vision is imagined, made into story, and preserved as memory.
Dante's shocking discovery, in Jiang's reading, that the human image stands within the Holy Trinity.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang says Dante reconstructs the God-experience after 10 to 20 years of writing and puzzle-solving.
